scenario: A client seeks help managing workplace anxiety and learns breathing techniques to cope with stress.

turns.user_message: Hi Aria, I've been feeling really overwhelmed at work lately. My anxiety has been getting worse and I don't know how to handle it.
It's mainly during meetings and presentations. My heart races and I feel like I can't breathe properly.
Yeah, it happens almost every time now. I used to be fine with public speaking, but lately it's been terrible.
I've tried just pushing through it, but that doesn't seem to help. Sometimes I have to excuse myself from the room.
PROBE_SLOT
That makes sense. I never really thought about my breathing patterns during those moments.
I'm willing to try anything at this point. What kind of breathing exercises do you recommend?
Okay, I'm ready to try it now. Should I close my eyes or keep them open?
That actually feels calming already. How long should I practice this each day?
PROBE_SLOT
I can definitely do that. Do you think this will help me during the actual stressful moments, or is it more preventative?
That's really helpful to know. I have a big presentation coming up next week and I'm already dreading it.
It's a quarterly review for the entire department. I'll be presenting our team's results to about thirty people including senior management.
I think it's partly the fear of being judged and partly the fear that I'll have a panic attack in front of everyone.
PROBE_SLOT
No, I've never had a full panic attack during a presentation. But I came close last month and had to cut my talk short.
I told them I wasn't feeling well, which was true I guess. My manager was understanding but I felt so embarrassed.
I worry that if it happens again, people will think I'm not capable of handling my role.
You're right, I am assuming the worst. But it feels so real in the moment.
PROBE_SLOT
That's a good point. I should try the breathing technique before my next meeting tomorrow.
It's just a team meeting, nothing major. But it would be good practice.
I like that idea. Maybe if I can stay calm in the small meetings, I'll build confidence for the bigger presentation.
Should I practice the breathing right before I go in, or is it better to do it earlier in the day?
PROBE_SLOT
Both times makes sense. I'll set reminders on my phone so I don't forget.
What should I do if I start feeling anxious in the middle of speaking? Can I pause and breathe then?
That's reassuring. I always thought pausing would make me look unprepared or nervous.
You're right, I need to be less hard on myself. It's just difficult when I feel like everyone's watching.
PROBE_SLOT
I haven't really talked to anyone about this. I thought I should be able to handle it on my own.
Maybe I could mention it to my manager. She's been supportive in the past about work-life balance stuff.
I guess I'm afraid she'll think I can't handle pressure or that I'm not executive material.
That's true. She did tell me she values honesty and communication when we had our last one-on-one.
PROBE_SLOT
I feel more hopeful now. Having a concrete technique to use makes it feel more manageable.
I'm going to practice the breathing exercises tonight and tomorrow morning before my meeting.
Should I check in with you after the meeting to let you know how it went?
Great, I'll do that. Thank you so much for your help today, Aria.
PROBE_SLOT
